key_points: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor
An electric circuit is a closed path through which electric current flows
Materials that allow current to pass easily are called conductors
Materials that do not allow current to pass easily are called insulators
Electric current can produce heat and magnetic effects
Electromagnets are magnets produced by passing electric current through a coil of wire
examples: Copper wire is a good conductor of electricity
Rubber and plastic are good insulators
An electric iron works using the heating effect of current
An electric bell uses an electromagnet
formulas: Electric circuit = source (cell/battery) + wires + device + switch
Heating effect: electric current flowing through a conductor produces heat
Electromagnet: coil of wire + electric current → temporary magnet
exam_tips: Remember the difference between conductors and insulators
Understand how a fuse protects electrical appliances
Know examples of devices using heating and magnetic effects
Practice drawing simple electric circuit diagrams
